threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of just 'the train'—everyone else knows it’s not special enough for that.
Last night on the 196, saw a guy break into a full Shakespeare monologue. No one batted an eye, just carried on scrolling on their phones.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw someone feeding a stray cat while arguing with a friend about the best Kebab in town—it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a club that doubles as a swimming pool. Dance all night, then take a dip at 4 AM. It's wild!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they grimace at a BART delay, yet still confidently navigate the bus detours like a GPS whisperer.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy doing yoga in the middle of the Muni aisle while we were stuck in traffic. Only in SF do you see that kind of calm amid chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think they can grab a quick bite at a bocadillo bar, but locals know the best sandwiches are from the little bakery near Plaça de la Vila.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they insist on ordering 'pan con tomate' at every meal, like it’s the holy grail of tapas.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: Calling it 'the Tube' without a hint of sarcasm is a dead giveaway. Locals know it's more of a gamble than a reliable transport option these days.
Last night on the 333, someone brought a saxophone and played 'Careless Whisper' while we waited at the lights. Strange but oddly comforting.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw my neighbor trading homemade sauerkraut for fresh veggies with the guy from two doors down at the corner café.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a late-night Kebab shop that also doubles as an art gallery. The walls are covered in local graffiti, a true cultural mash-up.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local knows to bring a jacket for those 'summer' evenings—even if it’s 75 during the day. The chill creeps in faster than tourists realize.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy playing guitar while his dog howled along on the 14. Muni is a stage, even when you’re stuck in traffic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we all love paella, but locals barely touch it. You’re more likely to find us arguing over the best pixel art in the streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their fascination with every single street performer. Locals just walk by, but newcomers stop for selfies with the saxophonist.
